headline: Access to justice index ranks Malaysia 47th globally
context: The World Justice Project Rule of Law Index 2025 ranked Malaysia 47th of 142 countries, with civil justice scoring lowest among all categories.

cards.big: Malaysia ranked 47th of 142 on WJP Rule of Law Index 2025
cards.sub: Civil justice accessibility scored 0.49, below regional average of 0.55

cards.big: Average civil court case takes 4.2 years from filing to resolution
cards.sub: High Court commercial cases resolved faster than family and labour disputes

cards.big: Article 8 guarantees equality before law but cost barriers persist
cards.sub: Average litigation cost of RM35,000 exceeds annual income of B40 households

cards.big: 72% of B40 households surveyed never consulted a lawyer in their lifetime
cards.sub: Legal literacy programs reach only 15% of rural communities

cards.big: Justice at RM35,000 per case is a service for the wealthy, not a right for all
Chief Justice launched e-Courts initiative to reduce backlog by 30% by 2026
